spec_match.rationale: Exercises the spec's semantic verbs (from, line, hline, vline, curve, close) across five presets, with parametric variation per preset via the slider. Outputs CSS that uses real shape() syntax including the curve...with...control-point form.

interactive.rationale: Five preset buttons + a parameter slider, live preview tile, copy-pasteable generated CSS, and a real CSS.supports probe for shape() detection.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises 'declarative geometry, semantic command names instead of raw <path>'; page delivers all five preset shapes with the named-verb syntax and a tweakable parameter.

edge_cases.rationale: CSS.supports probe correctly degrades for unsupported engines, but the slider has no validation for the bezier preset where extreme k values may produce self-intersecting curves; star preset's 0 value produces a degenerate inner radius.

references.rationale: see-also includes chromestatus.com/feature/5172258539307008 and the css-shapes-2 #shape-function spec URL directly.

design_system.rationale: Stage, preset-btn (with .active override of both background and color), code-out, controls all use design tokens; .ok and .bad use accent-emerald / accent-rose appropriately.

openQuestions.title: Add the move verb to a preset
openQuestions.detail: The spec includes `move` (lift-pen-then-place) which lets shape() express compound shapes (donut, C-shape). None of the five presets uses it. Add a sixth preset that demonstrates the move verb.

openQuestions.title: Clamp inputs to avoid degenerate shapes
openQuestions.detail: Bezier at t=1 gives k=70 which can produce loops. Star at extremes can give degenerate inner radius. Clamp the slider's effective range per preset, or add a 'spec-valid?' badge that flips when geometry would be self-intersecting.

openQuestions.title: Show the equivalent path() output
openQuestions.detail: The whole 'why shape() shipped' story is 'instead of raw path strings'. Add a sibling output panel that prints the equivalent path() / clip-path: path() string so visitors see the legibility win side-by-side.

openQuestions.title: Add a `by` keyword example
openQuestions.detail: The spec includes `to` (absolute) and `by` (relative) for line/curve. All five presets only use `to`. Adding a sixth preset using `by` would round out the verb coverage.

summary: Solid headline concept for the feature — five presets cover the main verbs and the slider parametrises each. To round it out, add move and by so the demo covers the full verb set, and consider a path() comparison.
